requirements.full_sentence:
Due diligence requirements with respect to LSPs
(1) Digital lending by a bank involving an LSP, shall be carried out under a
contractual agreement between the bank and the LSP, which clearly defines
the respective roles, rights, and obligations of each party thereto.
requirements.object: a contractual agreement between the bank and the LSP, which clearly defines the respective roles, rights, and obligations of each

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) A bank shall conduct enhanced due diligence before they enter into an
agreement with a LSP for digital lending, taking into account LSP's technical
capabilities, robustness of data privacy policies and storage systems, fairness
in conduct with borrowers, past records of conduct and ability to comply with
all applicable regulations and statutes.
requirements.object: before they enter into an agreement with a LSP for digital lending, taking into account LSP's technical capabilities, r

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) A bank shall carry out periodic review of the conduct of the LSP vis-à-vis the
terms of the contractual agreement and shall take appropriate action in the
event of any deviation therefrom.
requirements.object: of the conduct of the LSP vis-à-vis the terms of the contractual agreement and shall take appropriate action in the event of

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) A bank shall lay down, as part of its policy, suitable monitoring mechanisms for
the loan portfolios originated with the support of LSPs.
requirements.object: of its policy, suitable monitoring mechanisms for the loan portfolios originated with the support of LSPs

requirements.full_sentence:
(5) A bank shall impart necessary guidance to LSP acting as a recovery agent, to
discharge their duties responsibly and ensure that LSP complies with the
applicable instructions the Reserve Bank of India (Urban Co-operative Banks
- Responsible Business Conduct) Directions, 2025, and other relevant
instructions as issued from time to time.
requirements.object: LSP acting as a recovery agent, to discharge their duties responsibly and ensure that LSP complies with the applicable in

specified under the Reserve Bank of India (Urban Co-operative Banks -
Managing Risks in Outsourcing) Directions, 2025 and shall ensure that the
LSPs engaged by them and the DLAs (either of the bank or of the LSPs
engaged by the bank) comply with these Directions.
requirements.object: the extant guidelines on outsourcing as specified under the Reserve Bank of India (Urban Co-operative Banks - Managing Risks in

requirements.full_sentence:
Bank-LSP arrangements involving multiple lenders
In cases where a LSP has agreements with multiple lenders for digital lending,
each bank shall ensure the following:
(1) LSP shall provide a digital view of all the loan offers matching the borrower's
request on the DLA which meets the requirement of the borrower.
requirements.object: LSP shall provide a digital view of all the loan offers matching the borrower's request on the DLA which meets the requireme

requirements.full_sentence:
The name
of the unmatched lenders shall also be disclosed in the digital view.
requirements.object: the digital view

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) While the LSP may adopt any mechanism to match the request of borrowers
with the lender(s) to offer a loan, it shall follow a consistent approach for
similarly placed borrowers and products.
requirements.object: for similarly placed borrowers and products

requirements.full_sentence:
The mechanism adopted by the LSP
and any subsequent changes to this mechanism shall be properly
documented.

of the lender(s) extending the loan offer, amount and tenor of loan, APR,
monthly repayment obligation and penal charges (if applicable), in a way which
enables the borrower to make a fair comparison between various offers.

A link
to the KFS shall also be provided in respect of each of the lender.

However, ranking of loan offers based on a
publicly pre-disclosed metric for such ranking shall not be construed as
promoting a particular product.
requirements.object: a particular product

2(e) of the 'Guidelines for Prevention and Regulation of Dark Patterns, 2023'

requirements.full_sentence:
Assessing the borrower's credit worthiness
(1) A bank shall obtain the necessary information relating to economic profile of
the borrower with a view to assessing the borrower's creditworthiness before
extending any loan, including, at a minimum, age, occupation and income
details.
requirements.object: relating to economic profile of the borrower with a view to assessing the borrower's creditworthiness before extendin

requirements.full_sentence: The same shall be kept on record for audit purposes.
requirements.object: for audit purposes

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) A bank shall ensure that there is no automatic increase in credit limit unless an
explicit request is received, evaluated and kept on record from the borrower for
such increase.
requirements.object: no automatic increase in credit limit unless an explicit request is received, evaluated and kept on record from the borrower for

requirements.full_sentence:
Disclosures to borrowers
(1) A bank shall provide a Key Fact Statement (KFS), as per instructions contained
in the Reserve Bank of India (Urban Cooperative Banks - Responsible
Business Conduct) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: Statement (KFS), as per instructions contained in the Reserve Bank of India (Urban Cooperative Banks - Responsible Business Conduct

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) As regards penal charges, a bank shall be guided by the Reserve Bank of India
(Urban Cooperative Banks - Responsible Business Conduct) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: Reserve Bank of India (Urban Cooperative Banks - Responsible Business Conduct) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) A bank shall ensure that digitally signed documents (on the letter head of the
bank) viz., KFS, summary of loan product, sanction letter, terms and

requirements.object: documents (on the letter head of the bank) viz

requirements.full_sentence:
Loan disbursal, servicing and repayment
(1) Disbursement of loan by a bank shall always be made into the bank account
of the borrower except for disbursals covered exclusively under statutory or
regulatory mandate (of the Reserve Bank or of any other regulator) and
disbursals for specific end use, provided the loan is disbursed directly into the
bank account of the end-beneficiary.
requirements.object: the bank account of the borrower except for disbursals covered exclusively under statutory or regulatory mandate (of the Reserve Bank or of any other regulator) and disbursals for specific end use

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall ensure that in no case,
disbursal is made to a third-party account, including the accounts of LSP,
except as provided for in this Chapter.
requirements.object: case, disbursal is made to a third-party account, including the accounts of LSP

conditions: Provided that advances against salary

where the loan is disbursed directly to
the bank account of the borrower
requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that advances against salary, where the loan is disbursed directly to
the bank account of the borrower, but the repayment is from the corporate
employer, can be allowed subject to the condition that the loan is repaid by the
corporate employer by deducting the amount from the borrower's salary.

However, it must be ensured that LSPs do not have any control over the flow
of funds directly or indirectly in such transactions and that repayment is directly
from the bank account of the employer to the bank.
requirements.object: do not have any control over the flow of funds directly or indirectly in such transactions and that repayment is directly from th

requirements.object: servicing, repayment, etc

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) The flow of funds between the bank accounts of the borrower and the bank
shall not be controlled either directly or indirectly by a third-party, including the
LSP.
requirements.object: or indirectly by a third-party, including the LSP

requirements.full_sentence: (4) A bank shall ensure that any fees, charges, reimbursements, etc.
requirements.object: charges, reimbursements, etc

conditions: In case of delinquent loans
requirements.full_sentence:
(5) In case of delinquent loans, a bank may deploy physical interface to recover
loans in cash, wherever necessary.
requirements.object: recover loans in cash

requirements.full_sentence:
However, any recovery by cash shall
be duly reflected in full in the borrower's account on the same day and bank
shall ensure that any fees, charges, etc., payable to LSPs for such recovery
are paid directly by the bank and are not charged by LSP to the borrower either
directly or indirectly from the recovery proceeds.
requirements.object: full in the borrower's account on the same day and bank shall ensure that any fees, charges, etc

requirements.full_sentence:
Cooling-off period
(1) The borrower shall be given an explicit option to exit a digital loan by paying
the principal and the proportionate APR without any penalty during an initial
"cooling-off period".
requirements.object: option to exit a digital loan by paying the principal and the proportionate APR without any penalty during an initial "cooling-of

requirements.full_sentence:
The cooling off period shall be determined by the Board of
the bank in terms of their credit policy, subject to the period so determined not
being less than one day.
requirements.object: Board of the bank in terms of their credit policy

requirements.full_sentence:
For borrower continuing with the loan even after
cooling-off period, pre-payment shall continue to be allowed as per the
Reserve Bank of India (Urban Cooperative Banks - Responsible Business
Conduct) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: as per the Reserve Bank of India (Urban Cooperative Banks - Responsible Business Conduct) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) A bank may retain a reasonable one-time processing fee, if the customer exits
the loan during the cooling-off period.
requirements.object: processing fee

This, if applicable, shall be disclosed to
the customer upfront in KFS.
requirements.object: customer upfront in KFS

requirements.full_sentence:
Grievance redressal
(1) A bank, and the LSP which has an interface with the borrower, shall designate
nodal grievance redressal officers to deal with digital lending related
complaints/ issues raised by the borrower.
requirements.object: officers to deal with digital lending related complaints/ issues raised by the borrower

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Contact details of the nodal grievance redressal officers shall be prominently
displayed on the websites of the bank, its LSP and on the DLA, as well as in
the KFS provided to the borrower.
requirements.object: the websites of the bank, its LSP and on the DLA, as well as in the KFS provided to the borrower

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) The facility of lodging complaint shall also be made available on the DLA and
on the website as stated above.
requirements.object: on the DLA and on the website as stated above

requirements.full_sentence:
It is reiterated that responsibility of grievance
redressal shall continue to remain with the bank.
requirements.object: the bank

requirements.full_sentence:
This
information shall be suitably conveyed to the borrower.
requirements.object: the borrower

requirements.full_sentence:
Collection, usage and sharing of data with third parties
(1) A bank shall ensure that any collection of data by their DLA and DLA of their
LSP is need-based and with prior and explicit consent of the borrower having
audit trail.
requirements.object: of data by their DLA and DLA of their LSP is need-based and with prior and explicit consent of the borrower having audit tr

requirements.full_sentence:
In any case, a bank shall also ensure that DLA of bank/LSP desist
from accessing mobile phone resources like file and media, contact list, call
logs, telephony functions, etc.
requirements.object: of bank/LSP desist from accessing mobile phone resources like file and media, contact list, call logs, telephony functions, etc

conditions: if required
requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The borrower shall be provided with an option to give or deny consent for use
of specific data, restrict disclosure to third parties, data retention, revoke
consent already granted to collect personal data and if required, make the
bank/LSP delete/ forget the data.
requirements.object: option to give or deny consent for use of specific data, restrict disclosure to third parties, data retention, revoke consent alre

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) Explicit consent of the borrower shall be taken before sharing personal
information with any third party, except for cases where such sharing is
required as per statutory or regulatory requirement.
requirements.object: personal information with any third party

requirements.full_sentence:
Storage of data
(1) A bank shall ensure that LSP engaged by them do not store personal
information of borrower except some basic minimal data (viz., name, address,
contact details of the customer, etc.) that may be required to carry out their
operations or service within the scope of the bank-LSP agreement.
requirements.object: by them do not store personal information of borrower except some basic minimal data (viz

requirements.full_sentence:
Responsibility regarding data privacy and security of the customer's personal
information on an ongoing basis shall be that of the bank.
requirements.object: bank

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) A bank shall ensure that clear policy guidelines regarding the storage of
customer data including the type of data that can be stored, the length of time
for which data can be stored, restrictions on the use of data, data destruction
protocol, standards for handling security breach, etc., are put in place and also
disclosed by the bank and the LSP engaged by the bank prominently on their
website and DLA at all times.
requirements.object: guidelines regarding the storage of customer data including the type of data that can be stored, the length of time for which

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) A bank shall ensure that no biometric data is stored/ collected by the bank and
LSP, unless allowed under extant statutory guidelines.
requirements.object: data is stored/ collected by the bank and LSP

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) A bank shall ensure that all data is stored only in servers located within India,
while ensuring compliance with statutory obligations/ regulatory instructions.
requirements.object: is stored only in servers located within India, while ensuring compliance with statutory obligations/ regulatory instructions

conditions: in case the data is processed outside India
requirements.full_sentence:
Further, in case the data is processed outside India, the same shall be deleted
from servers outside India and brought back to India within 24 hours of
processing.
requirements.object: outside India and brought back to India within 24 hours of processing

requirements.full_sentence:
Comprehensive privacy policy
(1) 1) A bank and LSPs engaged by the bank shall have a comprehensive privacy
policy compliant with applicable laws, associated regulations and RBI
guidelines which shall be made available publicly on the website of the bank
and LSP, as the case may be.
requirements.object: policy compliant with applicable laws, associated regulations and RBI guidelines which shall be made available publicly o

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) 2) Details of third parties (where applicable) allowed to collect personal
information through the DLA shall also be disclosed in the privacy policy.
requirements.object: the privacy policy

requirements.full_sentence:
Technology standards
(1) A bank shall ensure that they and the LSPs engaged by them comply with
various technology standards/ requirements on cybersecurity stipulated by RBI
and other relevant agencies, or as may be specified from time to time, for
undertaking digital lending.
requirements.object: the LSPs engaged by them comply with various technology standards/ requirements on cybersecurity stipulated by RBI and other rele

requirements.full_sentence:
CIC Regulations, 2006 and related guidelines
issued by the Reserve Bank from time to time, a bank shall ensure that any
lending done through their DLAs and/ or DLAs of LSPs is reported by them to
CICs irrespective of its nature/ tenor.

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall ensure that LSPs, if any, associated with such
deferred payment credit products shall abide by the extant outsourcing
guidelines issued by the Reserve Bank and be guided by these Directions.

Reporting of DLAs to RBI
(1) A bank shall report all DLAs deployed/ joined by them, whether their own or
those of the LSPs, either exclusively or as a platform participant, on the
Centralised Information Management System (CIMS) portal of the Reserve
Bank in the requisite format as given in the Annex-I to these Directions.
requirements.object: joined by them, whether their own or those of the LSPs, either exclusively or as a platform participant, on the Centralised

(2) A bank shall update the aforesaid list as and when additional DLA(s) are
deployed or the engagement with the existing DLA(s) ceases to exist by filing
the updated data in the CIMS portal.
requirements.object: as and when additional DLA(s) are deployed or the engagement with the existing DLA(s) ceases to exist by filing the updated

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) The Chief Compliance Officer of the bank or any other official designated by
the Board of the bank for the purpose shall certify that the data on DLAs
submitted by them on the CIMS portal is correct and the DLAs are compliant
with all the extant regulatory instructions, including the provisions of this

requirements.object: on DLAs submitted by them on the CIMS portal is correct and the DLAs are compliant with all the extant regulatory instructions,

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) Without prejudice to the generality of the above, the Chief Compliance Officer/
other official designated by the Board of the bank shall certify the following
aspects:

(5) A bank shall ensure the correctness and timeliness of information regarding
DLAs, as the data, as submitted by the bank on CIMS, shall be published on
the website of the Reserve Bank in an automated manner and the Reserve
Bank shall not verify/ validate the data submitted on CIMS.
requirements.object: submitted on CIMS

requirements.full_sentence:
All issues and
grievances of customers concerning DLAs shall be addressed and dealt with
by the bank directly.
requirements.object: with by the bank directly

requirements.full_sentence:

requirements.object: DLAs or any associated entity as conferring any form of registration, authorization, or endorsement by the Reserve Bank

inclusion is not misrepresented in any marketing, promotional, or other

requirements.full_sentence:
Eligibility as DLG provider
(1) A bank may enter into DLG arrangements only with an LSP/ other lender
engaged as an LSP.
requirements.object: only with an LSP/ other lender engaged as an LSP

requirements.full_sentence:
Further, the LSP providing DLG shall be incorporated as
a company under the Companies Act, 2013.
requirements.object: company under the Companies Act, 2013

conditions: if any, payable to/ received by the DLG provider
requirements.full_sentence:
Due diligence and other requirements with respect to DLG provider
(1) A bank, including a bank acting as DLG provider, shall lay down, as part of its
policy, the eligibility criteria for DLG provider, nature and extent of DLG cover,
process of monitoring and reviewing the DLG arrangement, and the details of
the fees, if any, payable to/ received by the DLG provider, as the case may be
before entering into any DLG arrangement.
requirements.object: of its policy, the eligibility criteria for DLG provider, nature and extent of DLG cover, process of monitoring and reviewing the DLG arrangement, and the details of the fees

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) A bank shall ensure that any DLG arrangement does not act as a substitute for
credit appraisal requirements and robust credit underwriting standards need to
be put in place irrespective of the DLG cover.
requirements.object: arrangement does not act as a substitute for credit appraisal requirements and robust credit underwriting standards need to be put

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) Every time a bank enters into or renews a DLG arrangement, it shall obtain
adequate information to satisfy itself that the entity extending DLG would be
able to honour it.
requirements.object: satisfy itself that the entity extending DLG would be able to honour it

requirements.full_sentence:
Such information shall, at a minimum, include a declaration
from the DLG provider, certified by the statutory auditor of the DLG provider,
on the aggregate DLG amount outstanding, the number of lenders and the
respective number of portfolios against which DLG has been provided.
requirements.object: include a declaration from the DLG provider, certified by the statutory auditor of the DLG provider, on the aggregate DLG amount outst

requirements.full_sentence:
The
declaration shall also contain past default rates on similar portfolios.
requirements.object: rates on similar portfolios

requirements.full_sentence:
Restrictions on entering into DLG arrangements
(1) A bank shall not enter into DLG arrangements for revolving credit facilities
offered through digital lending channel and credit cards as defined under the
Reserve Bank of India (Urban Co-operative Banks - Credit Cards and Debit
Cards: Issuance and Conduct) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: for revolving credit facilities offered through digital lending channel and credit cards as defined under the Reserve Bank

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) A bank shall not enter into DLG arrangements on the loans which are covered
by the credit guarantee schemes administered by trust funds as specified
under the Reserve Bank of India (Urban Co-operative Banks - Prudential
Norms on Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: on the loans which are covered by the credit guarantee schemes administered by trust funds as specified under the Reserve

requirements.full_sentence:
Structure of DLG arrangements
(1) DLG arrangements shall be backed by an explicit and legally enforceable
contract between a bank and the DLG provider.
requirements.object: explicit and legally enforceable contract between a bank and the DLG provider

requirements.full_sentence:
Such contract, among other
things, shall contain the following details:

Forms of DLG
(1) A bank shall accept DLG only in one or more of the following forms:
requirements.object: one or more of the following forms:

requirements.full_sentence:
Cap on DLG
(1) A bank shall ensure that the total amount of DLG cover on any outstanding
portfolio which is specified upfront shall not exceed five per cent of the total
amount disbursed out of that loan portfolio at any given time.
requirements.object: of the total amount disbursed out of that loan portfolio at any given time

conditions:
In case of implicit
guarantee arrangements
requirements.full_sentence:
In case of implicit
guarantee arrangements, the DLG Provider shall not bear performance risk of
more than the equivalent amount of five per cent of the underlying loan
portfolio.
requirements.object: more than the equivalent amount of five per cent of the underlying loan portfolio

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The portfolio over which DLG can be offered shall consist of identifiable and
measurable loan assets which have been sanctioned (the 'DLG set').
requirements.object: measurable loan assets which have been sanctioned (the 'DLG set')

requirements.full_sentence:
This
portfolio shall remain fixed for the purpose of DLG cover and is not meant to
be dynamic.
requirements.object: purpose of DLG cover and is not meant to be dynamic

requirements.full_sentence:
This portfolio shall remain "frozen" for the purpose of the specific DLG

negotiating / rediscounting of genuine commercial / trade bills:
(1) Banks shall sanction working capital limit as also bills limit to borrowers after
proper appraisal of their credit needs and in accordance with the Board
approved policy.
requirements.object: guidelines while purchasing / discounting / negotiating / rediscounting of genuine commercial / trade bills: (1) Banks shall s

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) UCBs shall clearly lay down a bill discounting policy approved by their Board
of Directors, which shall be consistent with their policy of sanctioning of working
capital limits.
requirements.object: bill discounting policy approved by their Board of Directors, which shall be consistent with their policy of sanctioning of working

requirements.full_sentence:
In this case, the procedure for Board approval shall include
banks' core operating process from the time the bills are tendered till these are
realised.
requirements.object: process from the time the bills are tendered till these are realised

requirements.full_sentence:
UCBs may review their core operating processes and simplify the
procedure in respect of bills financing.
requirements.object: processes and simplify the procedure in respect of bills financing

requirements.full_sentence:
In order to address the problem of delay
in realisation of bills, UCBs shall take advantage of improved computer /
communication network like Structured Financial Messaging System (SFMS),
wherever available, and adopt the system of 'value dating' of their clients'
accounts.
requirements.object: computer / communication network like Structured Financial Messaging System (SFMS)

requirements.full_sentence:
UCBs shall open letters of credit (LCs) and purchase / discount / negotiate bills
under LCs only in respect of genuine commercial and trade transactions of their
borrower constituents who have been sanctioned regular credit facilities by the
banks.
requirements.object: (LCs) and purchase / discount / negotiate bills under LCs only in respect of genuine commercial and trade transactions of their

requirements.full_sentence:
UCBs shall not, therefore, extend fund based (including bills financing) or
non-fund based facilities like opening of LCs, providing guarantees and
acceptances to non-constituent borrower or / and non-constituent member of a
consortium / multiple banking arrangement.
requirements.object: based (including bills financing) or non-fund based facilities like opening of LCs, providing guarantees and acceptances to n

conditions:
in case of bills drawn under LCs restricted to
a particular UCB
requirements.full_sentence:
With effect from March 30, 2012, in case of bills drawn under LCs restricted to
a particular UCB, and the beneficiary of the LC is not a borrower who has been
granted regular credit facility by that UCB, the UCB concerned may, as per their
discretion and based on their perception about the credit worthiness of the LC
issuing bank, negotiate such LCs, subject to the condition that the proceeds will
be remitted to the regular banker of the beneficiary of the LC.
requirements.object: discretion and based on their perception about the credit worthiness of the LC issuing bank, negotiate such LCs

requirements.full_sentence:
In the
case of negotiations 'under reserve' the exposure shall be treated as on the
borrower and risk weight assigned accordingly.
requirements.object: the borrower and risk weight assigned accordingly

requirements.full_sentence:
While purchasing / discounting / negotiating bills under LCs or otherwise, UCBs
shall establish genuineness of underlying transactions / documents.
requirements.object: transactions / documents

conditions:
in case of
security items like blank cheques
requirements.full_sentence:
UCBs shall ensure that blank LC forms are kept in safe custody as in case of
security items like blank cheques, demand drafts etc.
requirements.object: forms are kept in safe custody as in case of security items like blank cheques, demand drafts etc

requirements.full_sentence:
LC forms shall be issued to customers under joint signatures of the
bank's authorised officials.
requirements.object: under joint signatures of the bank's authorised officials

requirements.full_sentence:
The practice of drawing bills of exchange claused 'without recourse' and issuing
letters of credit bearing the legend 'without recourse' shall be discouraged because
such notations deprive the negotiating bank of the right of recourse it has against
the drawer under the Negotiable Instruments Act.
requirements.object: notations deprive the negotiating bank of the right of recourse it has against the drawer under the Negotiable Instruments

requirements.full_sentence:
UCBs shall not, therefore, open
LCs and purchase / discount / negotiate bills bearing the 'without recourse' clause.
requirements.object: and purchase / discount / negotiate bills bearing the 'without recourse' clause

requirements.full_sentence: Accommodation bills shall not be purchased / discounted / negotiated by banks.
requirements.object: / negotiated by banks

requirements.full_sentence:
The underlying trade transactions shall be clearly identified, and a proper record
thereof maintained at the branches conducting the bills business.
requirements.object: a proper record thereof maintained at the branches conducting the bills business

requirements.full_sentence:
UCBs should be circumspect while discounting bills drawn by front finance
companies set up by large industrial groups on other group companies.
requirements.object: bills drawn by front finance companies set up by large industrial groups on other group companies

requirements.full_sentence: Bills rediscounts should be restricted to usance bills held by other banks.
requirements.object: bills held by other banks

requirements.full_sentence:
UCBs
should not rediscount bills earlier discounted by NBFCs except in respect of bills
arising from sale of light commercial vehicles and two / three wheelers.
requirements.object: by NBFCs except in respect of bills arising from sale of light commercial vehicles and two / three wheelers

requirements.full_sentence:
UCBs may exercise their commercial judgment in discounting of bills of services
sector.
requirements.object: in discounting of bills of services sector

requirements.full_sentence:
However, while discounting such bills, UCBs shall ensure that actual
services are rendered, and accommodation bills are not discounted.
requirements.object: are rendered, and accommodation bills are not discounted

requirements.full_sentence:
Services
sector bills should not be eligible for rediscounting.

Further, providing finance
against discounting of services sector bills may be treated as unsecured advance
and therefore, should be within the limits prescribed by Reserve Bank of India for
sanction of unsecured advances.
requirements.object: advance and therefore, should be within the limits prescribed by Reserve Bank of India for sanction of unsecured advances

requirements.full_sentence:
In order to promote payment discipline which would to a certain extent
encourage acceptance of bills, all corporate and other constituent borrowers
having turnover above threshold level as fixed by the bank's Board of Directors
should be mandated to disclose 'aging schedule' of their overdue payables in their
periodical returns submitted to banks.
requirements.object: 'aging schedule' of their overdue payables in their periodical returns submitted to banks

requirements.full_sentence:
UCBs shall not finance NBFCs, other than those engaged in hire-purchase /
leasing, subject to obtaining prior approval of the Registrar of Co-operative
Societies concerned before admitting such leasing / hire purchase companies as
members for the purpose of lending.
requirements.object: those engaged in hire-purchase / leasing

requirements.full_sentence:
Within the prescribed credit exposure norms and above stated restrictions,
UCBs, with working capital funds aggregating to ₹25 crore and above, may finance
the NBFC - Investment and Credit Companies (NBFC-ICC), subject to the
following limits:
Type of NBFC Maximum Limit on
Bank Finance
Note
(1) The maximum limit on aggregate bank finance to an NBFC should be within
the overall ceiling of borrowing by NBFCs, upto ten times of their NOF.
requirements.object: Investment and Credit Companies (NBFC-ICC)

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Bank finance to leasing concerns should be restricted only to "full payout"
leases i.e., those leases where the cost of the asset is fully recovered during
the primary lease period itself and further it should cover purchases of only
new equipment.
requirements.object: "full payout" leases i

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) As a prudent policy, lease rentals due during the period of next five years
should alone be taken into account for the purpose of lending.
requirements.object: account for the purpose of lending

requirements.full_sentence:
As
such, these items should be excluded from the build-up of current assets while
arriving at permissible bank finance for all categories of NBFCs:
(1) Bills discounted / rediscounted by NBFCs, except where specifically permitted.
requirements.object: build-up of current assets while arriving at permissible bank finance for all categories of NBFCs: (1) Bills discounted / rediscounted by NBFCs

requirements.full_sentence:
In respect of items indicated at (i) and (ii) above, banks should not make any
adjustment in the projected net working capital (NWC).
requirements.object: the projected net working capital (NWC)

requirements.full_sentence:
It may be added that the
projected NWC represents long-term surplus available to support current
operations and, therefore, does not need to be adjusted as a result of changing /
pruning the level of current assets while reducing the level of maximum permissible
bank finance.
requirements.object: projected NWC represents long-term surplus available to support current operations and, therefore, does not need to be adjusted as a

requirements.full_sentence:
Scheduled UCBs may rediscount bills discounted by NBFCs arising from sale
of commercial vehicles, including light commercial vehicles, two wheeler and three
wheeler vehicles, subject to normal lending safeguards and the following
conditions:
(1) the bills should have been drawn by the manufacturers on dealers only.
requirements.object: NBFCs arising from sale of commercial vehicles, including light commercial vehicles, two wheeler and three wheeler vehicles

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) the bills should represent genuine sale transactions as may be ascertained
from the chassis / engine numbers.
requirements.object: chassis / engine numbers

requirements.full_sentence:
and,
(3) before rediscounting the bills, the UCBs should satisfy themselves about the
bona- fides and track record of NBFCs which have discounted the bills.
requirements.object: bona- fides and track record of NBFCs which have discounted the bills

requirements.full_sentence:
The UCBs should not circumvent these instructions by purport and / or intent
by sanction of credit under a different nomenclature like unsecured negotiable
notes, floating rate interest bonds, etc.
requirements.object: purport and / or intent by sanction of credit under a different nomenclature like unsecured negotiable notes, floatin

requirements.full_sentence:
UCBs
may therefore ensure that no loans are sanctioned for acquisition of / investing in
small savings instruments including KVPs.
requirements.object: loans are sanctioned for acquisition of / investing in small savings instruments including KVPs

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) The banks should maintain a balanced portfolio of equipment leasing, hire
purchase vis-a-vis aggregate credit.
requirements.object: of equipment leasing, hire purchase vis-a-vis aggregate credit

requirements.full_sentence:
Credit exposure to each of these activities
should not exceed 5 per cent of total advances.
requirements.object: of total advances

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) The banks undertaking these activities should follow prudent accounting
standards.
Entire lease rental should not be taken to banks' income account.
requirements.object: income account

requirements.full_sentence: Only the interest component should be taken to income account.
requirements.object: account

requirements.full_sentence:
The
component representing replacement cost of the asset should be carried to the
Balance Sheet in the form of a provision for depreciation.
requirements.object: Balance Sheet in the form of a provision for depreciation

requirements.full_sentence:
(5) As a prudent measure, full depreciation should be provided during the primary
lease period of the asset.
requirements.object: primary lease period of the asset

requirements.full_sentence:
(6) Non Scheduled UCBs, which also desire to undertake these activities should
obtain RBI permission.

UCBs are permitted to finance agricultural activities subject to the following
conditions:
(1) UCBs would provide direct finance only to members (no nominal members)
and not through any agency like primary agricultural credit societies and
primary land development banks etc.,
(2) Credit should be extended only after obtaining 'no dues certificate' from the
existing credit agencies in the area, and
(3) Banks should follow the scales of finance and obtain security as per guideline
issued by RBI / NABARD.
requirements.object: obtaining 'no dues certificate' from the existing credit agencies in the area, and (3) Banks should follow the scales of financ

requirements.full_sentence:
In the circumstances,
primary (urban) cooperative banks should safeguard their interest vis-à-vis such
statutory dues.
requirements.object: such statutory dues

requirements.full_sentence:
Therefore, banks should satisfy themselves that there are no arrears of
Provident Fund and other statutory dues of the borrowers by obtaining a
declaration from them that all such dues have been duly paid.
requirements.object: are no arrears of Provident Fund and other statutory dues of the borrowers by obtaining a declaration from them that all

requirements.full_sentence:
Proof in this regard
may be called for only in cases where banks have reason to doubt the borrowers'
declaration.
requirements.object: in cases where banks have reason to doubt the borrowers' declaration

requirements.full_sentence:
production of a receipt
evidencing the payment of the dues or a certificate from the auditors of the
borrower or any other similar proof may be considered sufficient.

conditions: where there are arrears for reasons beyond the control of the borrowers
requirements.full_sentence:
In the case of
sick units where there are arrears for reasons beyond the control of the borrowers,
banks may continue to consider such cases on merits.
requirements.object: cases on merits

requirements.full_sentence:
The assessment of working capital requirement of borrowers, other than micro
and small enterprises, requiring fund based working capital limits up to ₹1 crore
and micro and small enterprises requiring fund based working capital limits up to
₹5 crore from the banking system may be made on the basis of their projected
annual turnover.
requirements.object: basis of their projected annual turnover

requirements.full_sentence:
Projected turnover may be interpreted as 'Gross Sales' including
excise duty.
requirements.object: Sales' including excise duty

requirements.full_sentence:
The UCBs may, at their discretion, carry out the assessment based on projected
turnover basis or the traditional method.
requirements.object: carry out the assessment based on projected turnover basis or the traditional method

requirements.full_sentence:
If the credit requirement based on
traditional production / processing cycle is higher than the one assessed on
projected turnover basis, the same may be sanctioned, as borrower must be
financed up to the extent of minimum 20 per cent of their projected annual turnover.
requirements.object: must be financed up to the extent of minimum 20 per cent of their projected annual turnover

requirements.full_sentence:
Actual drawals may be allowed on the basis of drawing power
to be determined by UCBs after excluding unpaid stocks.
requirements.object: basis of drawing power to be determined by UCBs after excluding unpaid stocks

requirements.full_sentence:
Drawals against the limits should be allowed against the usual safeguards
including drawing power and it is to be ensured that the same are used for the
purpose intended.
requirements.object: usual safeguards including drawing power and it is to be ensured that the same are used for the purpose intended

requirements.full_sentence:
In respect of borrowers other than micro and small enterprises, requiring
working capital limits above ₹1 crore and for micro and small enterprises/units
requiring fund based working capital limits above ₹5 crore, UCBs may determine
the working capital requirements according to their perception of the credit needs
of borrowers.
requirements.object: requirements according to their perception of the credit needs of borrowers

requirements.full_sentence:
UCBs may adopt turnover method or cash budgeting method or any
other method as considered necessary.
requirements.object: cash budgeting method or any other method as considered necessary

requirements.full_sentence:
However, UCBs may ensure that the
book- debt finance does not exceed 75 per cent of the limits sanctioned to
borrowers for financing inland credit sales.
requirements.object: debt finance does not exceed 75 per cent of the limits sanctioned to borrowers for financing inland credit sales

requirements.full_sentence:
The remaining 25 per cent of the credit
sales may be financed through bills to ensure greater use of bills for financing
sales.
requirements.object: to ensure greater use of bills for financing sales

requirements.full_sentence:
The Directions,
instructions and guidelines already repealed shall continue to remain repealed.

Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been
taken, or initiated under the repealed Directions, instructions, or guidelines shall
continue to be governed by the provisions thereof.
requirements.object: by the provisions thereof

requirements.full_sentence:
All approvals or
acknowledgments granted under these repealed lists shall be deemed as
governed by these Directions.
requirements.object: by these Directions

requirements.full_sentence:
Further, the repeal of these Directions, instructions,
or guidelines shall not in any way prejudicially affect:
(1) any right, obligation or liability acquired, accrued, or incurred thereunder.
requirements.object: affect: (1) any right, obligation or liability acquired, accrued, or incurred thereunder

conditions: if those directions
requirements.full_sentence:
and any such investigation, legal proceedings or remedy may be instituted,
continued, or enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may be
imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guidelines had not been
repealed.
requirements.object: enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guideline

requirements.full_sentence:
The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation
of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time
being in force.
requirements.object: and not in derogation of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time being in force

conditions: if it considers necessary
requirements.full_sentence:
For the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of these Directions or in order
to remove any difficulties in the application or interpretation of the provisions of
these Directions, the RBI may, if it considers necessary, issue necessary
clarifications in respect of any matter covered herein and the interpretation of any
provision of these Directions given by the RBI shall be final and binding.
